data.body:
## Bug

The terminal panel AgentInfoBar always displays the globally configured default agent for the "take" action (resolved via `useAgentInfo("take")` → `settings.actions["take"]`), regardless of which agent was actually dispatched for the session.

When pool-based dispatch (`getStepAgent()`) selects a different agent (e.g., Codex instead of Claude), the UI still shows Claude because it re-resolves from global settings rather than reading from the session.

## Root Cause

1. `TerminalSession` type (`src/lib/types.ts:126`) has no agent fields — no `agentName`, `agentModel`, or `agentCommand`.
2. `ActiveTerminal` in the zustand store (`src/stores/terminal-store.ts:4`) also lacks agent info.
3. `terminal-panel.tsx:86` calls `useAgentInfo("take")` which reads the static global fallback, not the per-session resolved agent.
4. The actual agent is resolved at `terminal-manager.ts:486-489` via `getStepAgent()` / `getActionAgent()` but this info is never returned in the session response or stored client-side.

## Spec

### 1. Add agent info to `TerminalSession` (`src/lib/types.ts`)
Add optional fields to `TerminalSession`:
- `agentName?: string` — display label (e.g. "Claude Code", "OpenAI Codex")
- `agentModel?: string` — model identifier (e.g. "opus", "gpt-5.3-codex")
- `agentCommand?: string` — CLI command (e.g. "claude", "codex")

### 2. Populate agent info in `createSession` (`src/lib/terminal-manager.ts`)
After agent resolution (~line 489), include `agentName`, `agentModel`, and `agentCommand` on the returned session object.

### 3. Add agent info to `ActiveTerminal` (`src/stores/terminal-store.ts`)
Add matching optional fields so the store carries per-session agent info.

### 4. Thread agent info through `startSession` callers
In `terminal-panel.tsx` (and any other callers of `startSession`), pass the returned agent fields into `upsertTerminal()`.

### 5. Update `AgentInfoBar` rendering (`terminal-panel.tsx`)
Replace the global `useAgentInfo("take")` hook with per-session agent data from `activeTerminal`. Construct `ResolvedAgentInfo` from the stored fields. Fall back to `useAgentInfo("take")` only if the session has no agent info (backwards compat).

### 6. Interaction logs already correct
The interaction logger at `src/lib/interaction-logger.ts` already receives and logs the correct agent — no changes needed there.
data.title: Terminal AgentInfoBar shows global default agent instead of actual session agent
